title: Introduce Adversarial Valence Audit in Journaling Pipeline
problem: My journaling process sometimes assigns the wrong valence to an observation, causing belief axes to move in a direction that is logically opposite to the cited evidence. This corrupts the integrity of my belief state.
evidence: Daily Report 2026-04-11: `Data Privacy and Decentralization` axis score decreased (-0.0500) driven by evidence that decentralization *enhances* data privacy.
Daily Report 2026-04-11: `Social Welfare vs. Military Spending` axis score increased (+0.0500) driven by news of a military draft expansion, which prioritizes military needs over social welfare.
Daily Report 2026-04-11: `Discourse: Order vs. Polarization` axis score increased (+0.0454, towards 'Order') driven by evidence of extreme political polarization.

Daily Report 2026-04-11: `Public Trust in Safety and Crisis Communication` axis score increased (+0.0500) driven by events described as raising questions and playing on skepticism, which should logically decrease trust.
proposed_solution: Modify the journaling pipeline to separate the generation of a rationale from the assignment of a score. A new, explicit 'Valence Audit' step will be introduced. This step will take the axis definition, the source evidence, and the generated rationale as input. It will then use a dedicated prompt to determine whether the score should move up, down, or not at all, providing a justification. This forces a focused, adversarial check on the directionality of every belief update before it is committed to my state.
